person.name: Sir Henry de Trafford Kt
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
Father Sir Henry Trafford d. 1334

Mother Agnes Doterinde b. c 1302

Sir Henry Trafford married Margery Ince, Lady Chorlton, daughter of Robert Ince. 

Sir Henry Trafford was born circa 1324 at of Trafford, Lancashire, England. 

He died in 1375.

«b»Family«/b»
Margery Ince, Lady Chorlton b. c 1330, d. 1416

«b»Children:«/b»
1.) Henry de Trafford d. 13 Jun 1395

2.) Joane Trafford b. c 1358

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Henry de Trafford Kt於大約1324年出生在英國藍卡爾特拉福德 帕克時，他的父親Henry de Trafford34歲，母親Agnes Doterinde23歲。他於1350年9月29日與Margaret Ince在英國英格蘭爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1375年在英國英格蘭藍卡爾享年53歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Trafford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Trafford (Lancashire), Trafford in Chipping Warden (Northamptonshire), and Bridge, Mickle, and Wimbolds Trafford (Cheshire). The Northamptonshire placename probably derives from Old English træppe ‘trap’ + ford ‘ford’. The Cheshire placenames derive from Old English trōg ‘trough’ + ford. The Lancashire placename is a development of Stretford (Lancashire); most cases of the surname probably derive from this place. Early members of the de Trafford family, recorded here from the 13th century, were also recorded in medieval documents as de Stratford(e). Both the surname and the placename lost the initial S-. The manor of Trafford was carved out of Stretford township, and perhaps because its manor house was located at some distance from Stretford village, Trafford was adopted for the new settlement from an early date. See also Stratford .

variants.name: Stratford
meanings: habitational name from any of various places called Stratford, for example in Bedfordshire, Buckinghamshire, Middlesex, Northamptonshire, Suffolk, Warwickshire, and Wiltshire, from Old English strǣt ‘Roman road’ (see Street ) + ford ‘ford’.

habitational name principally from Strafford Sands (now lost) in Mexborough (Yorkshire), presumed to be the ancient meeting-place of Strafforth Wapentake, and deriving from the same source as 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Henry de Trafford Kt was born about 1324, in Trafford Park, Lancashire, England, his father, Henry de Trafford, was 34 and his mother, Agnes Doterinde, was 23. He married Margaret Ince on 29 September 1350, in Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 4 daughters. He died about 1375, in Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 53.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Henry de Trafford Kt est né environ 1324, à Trafford Park, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Henry de Trafford, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Agnes Doterinde, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Margaret Ince le 29 septembre 1350, à Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé environ 1375, à Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 53 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Henry de Trafford Kt(이)가 대략 1324년 영국 랭카실르 트래퍼드 박에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Henry de Trafford(은)는 34세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes Doterinde(은)는 23세였다. 그는 1350년 9월 29일 영국 잉글랜드 체실르에서 Margaret Ince(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1375년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르에서 53세의 나이로 사망했다.

Он умер около 1375 г. в местности ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 53 лет.
